title: Bad Sleep Leaves Scars on MRI Years Before Symptoms Show Up
description: Large neuroimaging studies show that abnormal sleep duration — too short or too long — leaves measurable white matter lesions on MRI years before any cognitive symptom appears. A 2026 randomized trial has now confirmed the biological mechanism in living humans for the first time. But the finding comes with a catch that changes everything: we still can't fully prove the damage isn't already happening before the bad sleep begins.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for Your Brain Is Already Scarred. The MRI Just Hasn't Lied to You Yet.
